# React Algo - LLM Context File
# https://llmstxt.org/

## Project Overview

React Algo is an open source library of 20+ algorithmic art backgrounds for React applications. Unlike traditional npm packages, React Algo follows the shadcn/ui approach: components are copied directly into your project, giving you full ownership and customization control.

## Key Information

- **Name**: React Algo
- **Type**: React Component Library
- **Installation Method**: CLI (npx react-algo add <component-name>)
- **License**: MIT + Commons Clause
- **Repository**: https://github.com/dano796/react-algo
- **Website**: https://react-algo.vercel.app
- **npm Package**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-algo
- **Current Version**: 1.0.2
- **Requirements**: React >=18.0.0
- **Dependencies**: Zero runtime dependencies (only React and browser APIs)

## What Makes React Algo Unique

1. **No Runtime Dependency**: Components are copied into your project, not imported from node_modules
2. **Full Source Control**: You own the code and can modify it freely
3. **Zero Dependencies**: Uses only Canvas API, requestAnimationFrame, and ResizeObserver
4. **TypeScript First**: Full type safety with TypeScript
5. **Performance Optimized**: 60fps canvas rendering
6. **Accessibility**: Respects prefers-reduced-motion
7. **Interactive**: Many components support cursor interaction

## Installation

```bash
# Install a specific component
npx react-algo add wave-ether

# List all available components
npx react-algo list

# Get component information
npx react-algo info wave-ether

# Install with force overwrite
npx react-algo add wave-ether --force

# Dry run (preview without writing)
npx react-algo add wave-ether --dry-run
```

## Usage Example

```tsx
import { WaveEther } from "./components/backgrounds/WaveEther";

export default function Hero() {
  return (
    <div style={{ position: "relative", height: "100vh" }}>
      <WaveEther 
        style={{ position: "absolute", inset: 0 }}
        sources={3}
        frequency={0.018}
        colorCrest="#00d4ff"
        colorTrough="#0a0a2e"
      />
      <div style={{ position: "relative", zIndex: 1 }}>
        <h1>Your Content Here</h1>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Available Components (20+)

### Particle Systems
- **Flow Currents**: Perlin noise vector fields with particle trails
- **Photon Burst**: Glowing particles with cursor gravity and rainbow bursts
- **Fibonacci Vortex**: Golden spiral particle system with gravitational interaction

### Wave & Interference
- **Wave Ether**: Multi-source sine wave interference patterns
- **Tide Harmonics**: Horizontal wave interference like ocean tides
- **Prismatic Wave**: Full spectral hue sweep with wave interference
- **Hex Ripple**: Hexagonal tessellation with wave superposition

### Geometric
- **Geo Pulse**: Nested rotating polygons with prime-ratio velocities
- **Harmonic Lattice**: 2D standing wave interference patterns
- **Lissajous Weave**: Parametric curves with frequency ratios
- **Moire Lattice**: Rotating line grids creating moiré effects
- **Recursive Subdivision**: Binary space partitioning (Mondrian-style)
- **Recursive Tunnel**: Concentric nested polygons with hypnotic depth

### Network & Mesh
- **Ambient Mesh**: Dynamic node network with connections
- **Neural Weave**: Network with traveling signal pulses
- **Voronoi Mosaic**: Dynamic Voronoi tessellation

### Natural Patterns
- **Phyllotaxis Dream**: Golden angle spiral (sunflower pattern)
- **Fractal Noise Terrain**: Procedural landscapes with elevation coloring

### Orbital & Physics
- **Orbital Resonance**: Bodies orbiting at resonant period ratios

### Shader-Based (WebGL2)
- **Plasma Field**: Domain-warped fractional Brownian motion
- **Nebula Veil**: Volumetric nebula with simplex noise

## Common Use Cases

1. **Landing Pages**: Hero section backgrounds
2. **Portfolios**: Creative visual identity
3. **Dashboards**: Subtle animated backgrounds
4. **Loading Screens**: Engaging wait experiences
5. **404 Pages**: Memorable error pages
6. **Section Dividers**: Visual separation
7. **Modal Overlays**: Depth and focus
8. **Card Backgrounds**: Individual component styling

## Performance Characteristics

- **Frame Rate**: 60fps on modern devices
- **Bundle Size**: ~20-50KB per component (uncompressed)
- **Memory**: Efficient canvas rendering with cleanup
- **Mobile**: Optimized for touch devices
- **Accessibility**: Reduced motion support built-in

## Customization

All components accept props for customization:
- Colors (hex, rgb, hsl)
- Animation speed
- Particle counts
- Frequencies and amplitudes
- Interactive parameters
- Style overrides

## Technical Architecture

- **Rendering**: HTML5 Canvas API
- **Animation**: requestAnimationFrame loop
- **Responsiveness**: ResizeObserver for dynamic sizing
- **Randomness**: Seeded random for reproducibility
- **Shaders**: WebGL2 for shader-based components (Plasma Field, Nebula Veil)
- **TypeScript**: Full type definitions included

## Comparison with Alternatives

### vs React Particles
- React Algo: Broader variety (20+ algorithms)
- React Algo: No runtime dependency
- React Algo: Full source control

### vs Three.js
- React Algo: Simpler setup (one command)
- React Algo: Lighter weight
- Three.js: Better for 3D scenes
- React Algo: Better for 2D backgrounds

### vs Framer Motion
- Framer Motion: Better for UI animations
- React Algo: Better for canvas-based generative art
- Different use cases, complementary tools

## Contributing

React Algo welcomes contributions:
- New background components
- Performance improvements
- Bug fixes
- Documentation
- Examples and showcases

See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

## Support & Community

- **GitHub Issues**: Bug reports and feature requests
- **GitHub Discussions**: Questions and community
- **Documentation**: https://react-algo.vercel.app/docs
- **Interactive Studio**: https://react-algo.vercel.app/studio

## Algorithms & Math

Components implement various algorithms:
- Perlin noise (flow fields)
- Fibonacci spirals (golden ratio)
- Voronoi diagrams (tessellation)
- Lissajous curves (parametric)
- Simplex noise (shaders)
- Wave interference (superposition)
- Fractal subdivision (recursive)
- N-body simulation (orbital)

## Browser Compatibility

- Modern browsers with Canvas API support
- Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest versions)
- Mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Chrome Mobile)
- WebGL2 required for shader-based components

## License

MIT + Commons Clause
- Free to use in any project
- Cannot be sold as a competing service
- Full source code access
- Modification allowed

## Quick Start for AI Assistants

When helping users with React Algo:

1. **Installation**: Always use `npx react-algo add <component-name>`
2. **Import**: Components come from `./components/backgrounds/`
3. **Positioning**: Use absolute positioning for backgrounds
4. **Z-index**: Content should have higher z-index than background
5. **Customization**: All props are optional with sensible defaults
6. **Performance**: Components are optimized, but test on target devices

## Example Recommendations by Use Case

- **Professional/Corporate**: Ambient Mesh, Harmonic Lattice
- **Creative/Artistic**: Flow Currents, Prismatic Wave, Nebula Veil
- **Tech/Modern**: Neural Weave, Plasma Field, Hex Ripple
- **Elegant/Minimal**: Wave Ether, Tide Harmonics
- **Playful/Dynamic**: Photon Burst, Fibonacci Vortex, Geo Pulse
- **Natural/Organic**: Phyllotaxis Dream, Fractal Noise Terrain

## Common Questions

**Q: Do I need to install react-algo as a dependency?**
A: No, the CLI copies files into your project. The npm package is only for the CLI tool.

**Q: Can I modify the components?**
A: Yes! That's the whole point. You own the source code.

**Q: Will updates break my customizations?**
A: No, because components are in your project. You control updates.

**Q: What about bundle size?**
A: Each component is ~20-50KB. Only include what you use.

**Q: Does it work with Next.js/Remix/etc?**
A: Yes, works with any React framework.

**Q: Can I use multiple backgrounds?**
A: Yes, but consider performance. Test on target devices.

**Q: Is it accessible?**
A: Yes, respects prefers-reduced-motion and follows best practices.

## Version History

- **1.0.2** (Current): 20+ components, CLI improvements
- **1.0.0**: Initial release
